structured.title: Family Prepares for Palm Sunday Church Service
structured.overview: The recording captures a Sunday morning at home as a family gets ready for church on Palm Sunday. One adult, who appears to be a pastor or service leader, talks with Rachel about recovering from E. coli, misplaced items (a watch and cables), and adjusting clocks for daylight saving time. They discuss sermon planning, guest speakers, and how to plan events by mentally “playing the video” of the day in advance. The main focus is organizing the church service: deciding not to preach directly on Palm Sunday to preserve time to preach on the cross, planning how Lois will handle the introduction, readings, kids’ song (a Hosanna song), announcements, and catechism segment, and coordinating graphics for a Tenebrae (not child‑friendly) service and Easter Sunday. The speaker leaves a detailed voicemail for Lois with instructions on these points and asks her to invite him and Rachel up during announcements so they can share about their recent trip to Cape Town and the Advance global conference. Together, he and Rachel outline what they will say: explaining what Advance is, showing a video, highlighting encouragement from being part of a global church network (e.g., One City Church in Durban, God’s Tribe in Tanzania, Monument Church in Washington DC), praying with brothers and sisters from other countries, and feeling personally encouraged and refreshed. In parallel, they manage practical tasks like resetting the home modem due to app connectivity issues, packing laptops and projector, warning about potential shoe blisters, and timing when to add photos to the service plan. The conversation ends with gently but firmly encouraging their child Jude to get dressed for church, think about what to wear, and respond more positively than just saying “no.”

action_items.description: Reset modem to previous network name and password
Reinstall old modem if network apps still do not work after reset

events.title: Palm Sunday Church Service and Cape Town Trip Update
events.description: Church service including announcements, catechism segment, and a slot where the speaker and Rachel share about their trip to Cape Town and the Advance global conference.

apps_results.content:
## Health and daily routine  
- Gut still recovering after recent E. coli  
- Clock and watch times adjusted for daylight savings  
- Getting family up, dressed, and ready for church  

## Planning for church service  
- Preparing laptop, projector, minimal equipment for Sunday  
- Deciding not to preach on Palm Sunday, focus on the cross  
- Considering kids’ Hosanna song and palm crosses for children  

## Instructions for service leader (Lois)  
- Ask Lois to mention Palm Sunday in introduction  
- Replace song with gospel reading if preferred  
- Use graphics to advertise Tenebrae and Easter Sunday services  

## Tenebrae and catechism details  
- Tenebrae notes on WhatsApp; emphasize not child‑friendly  
- Lois to possibly read key Tenebrae info during notices  
- Catechisms: revisit last week’s Q&A, then introduce this week’s  

## Cape Town trip presentation  
- Plan: Lois calls them up during announcements  
- Show video first, then Rachel intro and explanation  
- Share encouragement: part of global Advance network, bigger family  

## Global network and encouragement  
- Mention churches: God’s Tribe (Tanzania), One City (Durban), Monument (Washington DC)  
- Note other churches knew about God First Barry  
- Personal impact: refreshed, encouraged, first flight abroad in 12 years  

## Next steps  
- Reset home modem; revert if apps still fail  
- Add Cape Town photos to service plan when possible  
- Finalize talking points before leaving in about 20 minutes
